title: Mild Polish Rice Bowl
description: A gentle Polish-inspired rice bowl with fragrant mirepoix, rice vinegar glaze, and floral dill, perfect for meal prep.
instructions: Rinse the jasmine rice until the water runs clear.
Combine the rinsed rice and 1 cup of water in a saucepan, bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes until tender.
While the rice cooks, heat 1 tbsp of sesame oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add the diced carrot, celery, and onion to the skillet and sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in grated ginger and minced garlic, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in tamari soy sauce, rice vinegar, and maple syrup, stir to combine, and let the mixture simmer for 2 minutes.
Remove the skillet from heat, stir in toasted sesame seeds and chopped dill, seasoning with sea salt and black pepper to taste.
When the rice is ready, fluff it and divide it into four meal-prep containers, top each with the warm mirepoix sauce, let cool, then refrigerate or heat before serving.
